skill.name: requesting-code-review
skill.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
skill.summary: A workflow skill for requesting code reviews from a subagent, designed for subagent-driven development workflows
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external commands (git operations) and weak crypto patterns. Manual review confirms these are false positives. Git commands are hardcoded for legitimate code review workflow. No cryptographic code exists. No actual security risks identified.

content.value_statement: This skill provides a structured workflow for requesting code reviews from a subagent, helping catch issues before they cascade into larger problems during development.
actual_capabilities: Dispatch code-reviewer subagent with structured template
Capture git commit SHAs for diff review
Provide review checklist covering code quality, architecture, testing, and production readiness
Categorize issues by severity (Critical, Important, Minor)
limitations: Requires subagent capability to dispatch code-reviewer
Does not perform actual code review itself - acts as workflow orchestrator
Does not integrate with external code review tools like GitHub PRs
Placeholder values must be provided by user or external process

use_cases.title: Review After Task Completion
use_cases.description: Use after completing each task in subagent-driven development to catch issues early before they compound
use_cases.target_user: Developers using Claude Code or Codex for task-based development

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Quality Gate
use_cases.description: Run code review before merging to main branch to ensure production readiness
use_cases.target_user: Development teams practicing code review before merge

use_cases.title: Stuck on Complex Problem
use_cases.description: Request a fresh perspective when stuck on a complex bug or implementation challenge
use_cases.target_user: Individual developers needing a second opinion on their code

prompt_templates.title: Basic Code Review Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the requesting-code-review skill to review my recent changes. Base SHA: [commit], Head SHA: [commit], What was implemented: [description]
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple review after completing a task

prompt_templates.title: Review with Plan Reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Request code review for the feature I just built. Use requesting-code-review with plan reference: [plan document], Base: [sha], Head: [sha], Description: [what was built]

prompt_templates.title: Quick Post-Task Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the requesting-code-review workflow. Implementation: [brief description], Base SHA: [sha], Head SHA: [sha]

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Feature Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute requesting-code-review. Implemented: [full description of feature], Requirements: [spec document or plan], Base SHA: [starting point], Head SHA: [current state], Description: [summary]
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed review of major feature implementation

output_examples.input: Request code review for my login feature implementation
output_examples.output: The code review would be dispatched to subagent and return: Strengths section listing what's well done, Issues categorized by severity (Critical/Important/Minor) with file:line references, Recommendations for improvements, and Assessment with Ready to merge verdict
The subagent code-reviewer would examine git diff, check migrations, evaluate schema design, and provide categorized feedback with production readiness assessment

best_practices: Request review after EACH task in subagent-driven development to catch issues early
Always provide the git SHA range so the reviewer can see actual changes
Act on Critical issues immediately before proceeding
anti_patterns: Skipping review because the task seemed simple
Ignoring Critical issues and proceeding anyway
Not providing the git SHA range - leaving reviewer without context

faq.question: What is the requesting-code-review skill?
faq.answer: It is a workflow skill that dispatches a code-reviewer subagent to review your code changes against requirements, checking code quality, architecture, testing, and production readiness.

faq.question: Do I need to configure anything before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need to have the code-reviewer subagent available and provide git commit SHAs (base and head) for the diff review.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with GitHub pull requests?
faq.answer: No, this skill works with git commit SHAs locally. It does not integrate with external PR systems - it is for subagent-driven development workflows.

faq.question: How often should I request reviews?
faq.answer: Best practice is to request review after EACH task in subagent-driven development, and definitely before merging to main branch.

faq.question: What if I disagree with the review feedback?
faq.answer: You can push back with technical reasoning. Show code or tests that prove your implementation works, and request clarification if needed.

faq.question: Does this skill actually execute git commands?
faq.answer: The skill shows example git commands for getting SHAs. The actual git diff is performed by the dispatched code-reviewer subagent using the provided SHA values.
